2604.15840.title: CoEvolve: Training LLM Agents via Agent-Data Mutual Evolution
2604.15840.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning for LLM agents is typically conducted on a static data distribution, which fails to adapt to the agent's evolving behavior and leads to poor coverage of complex environment interactions. To address these challenges, we propose CoEvolve, an agent-data mutual evolution framework that enables LLM agents to improve through closed-loop, interaction-driven training. Specifically, CoEvolve extracts feedback signals such as forgetting and uncertainty from rollout trajectories to identify failure-prone interaction patterns, and utilizes them to guide LLM-based task synthesis. The synthesized tasks are validated through environment interaction and utilized to update the data distribution, enabling joint adaptation of the agent and its data. Extensive experiments on AppWorld and BFCL across Qwen2.5-7B, Qwen3-4B, and Qwen3-30B-A3B demonstrate consistent and significant improvements over strong base models, yielding absolute gains of 19.43%, 15.58%, and 18.14%, respectively.
2604.15840.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習對於 LLM 代理通常是在靜態數據分佈上進行，這無法適應代理不斷演變的行為，並導致對複雜環境互動的覆蓋不足。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了 CoEvolve，一個代理-數據互動演化框架，使 LLM 代理能夠通過閉環的、以互動驅動的訓練來改進。具體而言，CoEvolve 從回放軌跡中提取忘記和不確定性等反饋信號，以識別易失敗的互動模式，並利用這些模式來指導基於 LLM 的任務合成。合成的任務通過環境互動進行驗證，並用於更新數據分佈，使代理及其數據能夠共同適應。在 Qwen2.5-7B、Qwen3-4B 和 Qwen3-30B-A3B 上的 AppWorld 和 BFCL 進行的廣泛實驗顯示，相較於強大的基礎模型，取得了一致且顯著的改進，絕對增益分別為 19.43%、15.58% 和 18.14%。
2604.15840.author: Shidong Yang et.al.
2604.15840.authors: Shidong Yang, Ziyu Ma, Tongwen Huang, Yiming Hu, Yong Wang, Xiangxiang Chu
